title: Linux Package Management (APT)
description: Install, update, search, and remove software packages on Debian/Ubuntu systems using apt and dpkg.

tasks.prompt: Refresh the local package index from configured repositories.
tasks.explanation: 'apt update' downloads the latest package lists from repositories without upgrading installed packages.

tasks.prompt: Upgrade all installed packages to their latest available versions.
tasks.explanation: 'apt upgrade' installs newer versions of currently installed packages, prompting before changes.

tasks.prompt: Install the package 'htop' from the repositories.
tasks.hint: Use apt install: 'sudo apt install htop'
tasks.explanation: 'apt install' resolves dependencies and installs the specified package and any required libraries.

tasks.prompt: Remove the package 'apache2' but keep its configuration files.
tasks.hint: Use apt remove: 'sudo apt remove apache2'
tasks.explanation: 'apt remove' uninstalls the package binaries but leaves configuration files in /etc for potential reinstall.

tasks.prompt: Completely remove 'apache2' including all configuration files.
tasks.hint: Use apt purge: 'sudo apt purge apache2'
tasks.explanation: 'apt purge' removes the package and deletes its configuration files from the system.

tasks.prompt: Search the package repository for packages matching 'nginx'.
tasks.explanation: 'apt search' queries the local package index for packages whose names or descriptions match the keyword.

tasks.prompt: Display detailed information about the 'curl' package.
tasks.explanation: 'apt show' prints metadata including version, dependencies, description, and installed status.

tasks.prompt: Remove packages that were automatically installed as dependencies but are no longer needed.
tasks.explanation: 'apt autoremove' cleans up orphaned dependency packages, freeing disk space safely.
